seoTitle: How to Start and Join a Zoom Call From Slack
metaDescription: Launch instant Zoom meetings directly from Slack channels using the /zoom slash command after installing the official app.
intro: Jumping between tabs to start a quick video call breaks your workflow. This guide covers installing the official Zoom app for Slack, authorizing your account, and launching a call instantly using a slash command.

steps.heading: Open the App Marketplace
steps.body: From the left sidebar of your Slack workspace, launch the App Marketplace.

steps.heading: Search for Zoom
steps.body: Type Zoom into the search field and select the verified app listing.

steps.heading: Install and authorize
steps.body: Click 'Add to Slack', review the requested permissions, and click Allow to continue.

steps.heading: Sign in to your Zoom account
steps.body: Follow the redirect to enter your Zoom login credentials and accept the security consent screen.

steps.heading: Start a call with a slash command
steps.body: In any channel, type /zoom and press Enter to generate a live meeting join card for your team.

faq.question: Do all participants need a Zoom account to join?
faq.answer: No, teammates can join through the generated link without needing their own Zoom account, though hosting features vary by role.

faq.question: Can I set Zoom as my default call option in Slack?
faq.answer: Yes, workspace settings let you map the phone or video icon to automatically launch Zoom.

faq.question: Does the integration work in direct messages too?
faq.answer: Yes, the /zoom command works in both public channels and direct messages.

faq.question: What happens if my Zoom license doesn't support the meeting length?
faq.answer: The meeting will follow your Zoom account's plan limits, so check your subscription if calls are cutting off early.
